title: (PM REVIEW): G1 — decouple qty from strategy signals
body: S-026 G1 lands: strategies emit the trade idea (symbol/side/entry/sl/tp) only — no qty. Sizing moves into the per-account RiskManager in G2. Touches src/runtime/pipeline.py + src/units/strategies/vwap.py (live-mode invariant list). Work-PR is draft pending operator ack. Tests green; zero new failures vs main. Behavioural change is deferred to G2; this PR injects MAX_QTY as a transitional placeholder for safe_place_order so validation continues to work.

title: (PM REVIEW): G2 — position sizing in per-account RiskManager
body: S-026 G2 lands: RiskManager.position_size(pkg, balance_usd) is the only sizing site post-G2. Multi-account dispatch sizes per-account inside Coordinator.multi_account_execute. Operator-confirmed numbers committed to accounts.yaml: risk_pct=0.01, min_balance_usd=50, no max-position cap. Touches pipeline.py, coordinator.py, accounts/risk.py, accounts.yaml (live-mode invariant list). Stacked on G1 (#281); base=claude/decouple-position-sizing-2evhb. Tests green; zero new failures vs main.

title: (PM REVIEW): G3 — dynamic sizing (live balance + daily-loss budget)
body: S-026 G3 lands: (1) sizing now reads live balance via processor.get_account_balances inside Coordinator.multi_account_execute (cached per dispatch round); (2) RiskManager.position_size now floor-rounds (never overshoots cap); (3) new daily-loss-budget gate scales qty down (or refuses with qty=0) when a full SL hit would push daily_pnl past -max_daily_loss_usd. Touches risk.py + coordinator.py (live-mode invariant list). Tests green; zero new failures vs main.

title: (PM REVIEW): G4 — audit-log strategy attribution / BUG-033 (S-026 COMPLETE)
body: S-026 G4 lands + sprint COMPLETE/WRAPPED. Bounds the BUG-033 'unknown ×4' attribution drift: (1) defensive fallback default flipped from 'unknown' to 'multiplexed' (the actual production builder name) so aggregators don't bucket missing labels; (2) one-shot diagnostic logger.warning fires when an actionable signal still resolves via the safety default, capturing signal/meta/env keys so the next operator-side hourly cycle on the VM pinpoints the leak source via journalctl; (3) 8 regression tests pin the contract; (4) BUG-033 logged. Behavioural change is observability-only. Tests green; zero new failures vs main.

title: (PM REVIEW): vwap execution routing fix — multi_account_execute → execute_pkg (BUG-034)
body: Recurring hardening session #1 lands the VWAP execution gap fix from the 2026-05-02 hourly report. Pre-fix: every live VWAP/turtle_soup signal failed at BybitAPI.place(dry_run=False) with NotImplementedError — the error message itself named the fix. Post-fix: Coordinator.multi_account_execute resolves a per-account exchange client (bybit_client_for / binance_conn_for) and routes through execute_pkg(pkg, account_cfg, exchange_client=client, qty_override=sized_qty, dry_run=...). qty_override (new) preserves the live RiskManager-approved qty so daily-loss-budget state survives the routing layer. Live-mode missing creds now hard-fails with a diagnostic ping instead of silently flipping to dry-run. Per-account execution failures emit a Telegram-ready JSON to runtime_logs/pending_pings/ via the new src/runtime/execution_diagnostics.py — account / strategy / symbol / side / qty / reason. 6 new regression tests pin the contract end-to-end. Touches src/core/coordinator.py + src/units/accounts/execute.py + src/runtime/execution_diagnostics.py (live-mode invariant rule 3 list). Work-PR is draft pending operator merge. Tests green; zero new failures vs main.

title: (PM REVIEW): S-029 — 3 P0 fixes from architecture-audit-2026-05-02
body: Operator-driven wider audit ships three Tier-2 P0 fixes — review and merge in any order: (1) #311 PR1 — multi_account_execute now enforces accounts.yaml `strategies: [...]` filter (vwap signals stop landing in turtle_soup-only wallets and vice versa). (2) #312 PR2 — execute_pkg writes every live trade to trade_journal.db on submission (was only writing smoke tests; /last5 + /strategies + hourly-report were silently incomplete). (3) #313 PR3 — new liveness_watchdog runs hourly from src/main.py and pings if ≥ 5 actionable signals fired but 0 trades landed (closes the silence gap that hid BUG-034). Audit doc + CLAUDE.md § Architecture rules already merged in #310. All three behavioural changes move *toward* live correctness — no path is added that bypasses the per-account RiskManager. Tests green on every PR (4 + 6 + 11 new + regression-adjacent suites pass).

title: (PM REVIEW): S-030 PR1 — order_packages log table + writers + dispatch insert (P1-5)
body: S-030 PR1 ships the foundation for the open-trade monitor loop (PR2 next session). Adds trade_journal.db::order_packages table with (strategy_name, updated_at) and (status, updated_at) indexes — Rule 4's primary access path is 'package logs per strategy'. Three Database methods: insert_order_package + update_order_package + get_order_packages_by_strategy. Coordinator._log_new_order_package runs once per dispatch round inside multi_account_execute (before the per-account fan-out), generates pkg-<uuid12>, stamps the id on pkg.meta so per-account rows can reference it. Best-effort: journal failure logs a warning and dispatch continues. Touches src/core/coordinator.py + src/data_layer/database.py — Tier 2 per Live-mode invariant rule (3) even though behaviour is observability-only. 13 new tests + 119 regression-adjacent pass. Next session (S-030 PR2): strategy monitor() hook + heartbeat-driven loop that reads open packages, calls monitor, updates the row, and re-runs risk_manager.approve to decide whether to modify or close the live order.

title: (PM REVIEW): S-030 PR2 — strategy monitor() hook + break-even-SL helper (P1-4)
body: Strategy monitor() contract on every strategy module — vwap and turtle_soup both delegate to a shared `monitor_breakeven_sl` helper in _base.py that returns {sl: entry} when a trade has captured 1R, otherwise None. Defensive: every malformed input returns None, never raises. 21 new tests cover the contract end-to-end; 94 regression-adjacent pass. Tier 2 per Architecture rule 2 (strategy unit changes). PR is contract-only — the monitor() hook is unused until PR3 builds the heartbeat-driven loop, so risk is zero behavioural change today. PR3 (next session) wires monitor() into a runtime loop that reads open packages, calls monitor, and on changes updates the DB + modifies the live exchange order via the account unit (re-runs risk_manager.approve).

title: (PM REVIEW): S-030 PR3 — order_monitor loop + close-side trade-row update (P1-4)
body: Completes the open-trade monitor stack (S-030). PR1 (#315) shipped the order_packages log; PR2 (#317) shipped the strategy monitor() hook; this PR (PR3) ships the loop that ties them together. New src/runtime/order_monitor.py runs once per pipeline tick, reads open packages, calls each strategy's monitor() with fresh candles, and applies non-None verdicts (sl/tp updates, close decisions) to the DB. New Database.update_trade closes the linked trades row on a close verdict. 15 new tests + 137 regression-adjacent pass. Tier 2 — touches src/main.py. Exchange-side modify/close is deferred to a small follow-up PR (account.update_open_trade + account.close_open_trade routing through execute_pkg's exchange client) — this PR is observability + DB-side bookkeeping only, so risk is zero behavioural change to live orders. After this merges + the follow-up: live order lifecycle is architecturally complete (signal → package → dispatch → trade → monitor → close, all logged).

title: (PM REVIEW): S-030 PR4 — exchange-side modify/close + monitor wiring (P1-4)
body: Completes the live order lifecycle. Strategy.monitor() verdicts already update the DB (PR3); this PR adds the exchange-side wiring so the live Bybit order is also modified or closed. Env-gated on MONITOR_APPLY_TO_EXCHANGE=true (default off — shadow mode preserved). New helpers: src/units/accounts/execute.py::modify_open_order (Bybit set_trading_stop wrapper) + close_open_position (reduce-only market close). New monitor bridges: _send_modify_to_exchange + _send_close_to_exchange resolve per-account Bybit client and dispatch. 29 new tests cover the 3 helpers + env-flag parsing + monitor integration. 113 regression-adjacent pass. Tier 2 — touches src/units/accounts/execute.py + src/runtime/order_monitor.py. After this merges + the env flag is flipped (small operator rollout notebook is the next session), the live order lifecycle is architecturally complete: signal → package → dispatch → trade → monitor (DB + exchange) → close (DB + exchange). Then the remaining sprints (S-031 → S-035) are pure boundary cleanup with no behavioural changes.

title: (PM REVIEW): S-031 PR4 — /closeall → processor.close_open_positions (Tier 2, P1-6 + Rule-3)
body: Closes the last Rule-3 violation in the bot. Pre-PR cmd_closeall called dl.close_all_bybit_positions_for_strategy which placed reduce-only market orders directly on Bybit, bypassing execute_pkg's single-entry contract for live order placement. Post-PR the bot calls processor.close_open_positions(strategy=..., account=...) which reads open trades from trade_journal.db, dispatches each close through src.units.accounts.execute.close_open_position (the canonical helper added in S-030 PR4 for the monitor loop's close path), and marks the trade row status='closed' on success. Both the typed /closeall <strategy> path and the inline-keyboard 'Close ALL' callback now route through the helper. 16 new tests cover empty scope, filter shape (strategy case-insensitive, account exact, combined), dispatch through the canonical path (verifies execute.close_open_position is called and client.place_order is NOT called by the helper), and per-trade failure isolation. Tier 2 — touches close routing (the caller, not the helper itself). src/units/accounts/execute.py is NOT modified in this PR; only the UI processor + bot handler change. Work-PR is draft pending operator merge. After this merges, S-031 PR5 (catch-all bot file-read handlers → UI helpers) and S-032 → S-035 are pure Tier-1 boundary cleanup.

title: Architecture compliance sprint COMPLETE (95%) — S-031 PR3-5, S-032, S-033, S-034 all merged; only S-035 left
body: Six of seven planned PRs landed this session. All six architecture rules from CLAUDE.md are now enforced in code: unit separation (Rule 1) — only the cosmetic folder layout in S-035 remains; strategies pure (Rule 2) — fetch_candles extracted in S-033; account/risk/execute (Rule 3) — /closeall now routes through execute.close_open_position pending operator merge of #327; DB unit owns three logs (Rule 4) — signals SQL table + dual-writer in S-034; bot is a thin shell (Rule 5) — every business-logic handler now goes through processor; live-by-default + watchdog (Rule 6) — already in CP-32/33. Open items: (1) #327 (S-031 PR4) draft pending operator merge — Tier 2 close-routing change. (2) S-035 (folder reshuffle, src/data_layer→src/units/db, src/ui→src/units/ui) deferred to next session per the audit prompt — 'huge diff, schedule for low-traffic window.' (3) S-034 follow-up cutover (flip readers, delete JSONL) trivial diff once operator confirms one full day of clean dual-writes. PRs: #326, #329, #330, #331, #332 (Tier 1 self-merged) + #327 (Tier 2 draft) + #328 (ping for #327). 72 new tests this session; ~150 regression-adjacent passes.

title: Architecture compliance sprint COMPLETE / WRAPPED — audit fully closed (S-031 → S-035 all merged)
body: All seven planned PRs landed: S-031 PR3 (#326), S-031 PR4 (#327, operator-approved), S-031 PR5 (#329), S-032 (#330), S-033 (#331), S-034 (#332), S-035 (#334). Plus ops PRs #328 (ping for #327) and #333 (CP-34 mid-sprint checkpoint). All six architecture rules from CLAUDE.md are now enforced in code AND in folder layout: (1) every unit lives under src/units/ — S-035 moved DB and UI; (2) strategies are pure — S-033 extracted fetch_candles; (3) account/risk/execute owns placement — S-031 PR4 routed /closeall through execute.close_open_position; (4) DB unit owns three logs — trades, order_packages (S-030), signals (S-034); (5) bot is a thin shell — S-031 PR1-5 pulled every business-logic handler into processor; (6) live by default + watchdog — S-029 PR3. The repo is fully boundary-clean. Only follow-up is the trivial S-034 cutover after the operator confirms one full day of clean SQL dual-writes (flip readers, delete JSONL). 78 new tests this sprint; ~150 regression-adjacent passes. No regressions; the pre-existing _bybit_client failures in test_data_loaders.py existed on every prior commit. Lessons learned (CP-35 § 7): (a) module-aliasing shim is the magic carpet for folder moves; (b) inject dependencies into helpers, don't construct in-place; (c) dual-writer + env escape hatch is the pattern for logging migrations; (d) the audit-first sprint shape (one doc enumerates findings, one PR per finding, all Tier-1 self-merge except where the audit explicitly says Tier 2) lets a single session close 95% of an architecture audit; (e) clean __pycache__ before git-mv'ing a folder.
